paleo-style-breakfast-sausage.name: Paleo-style Breakfast Sausage
instructions: Mix ground pork, sage, thyme, salt, pepper, rosemary, nutmeg, cayenne pepper, and red pepper flakes in a bowl with your hands until thoroughly combined. Form into 8 patties.

Working in batches, cook patties in a large skillet over medium heat until browned, 8 to 10 minutes. Flip patties and continue to cook until browned on the other side, 5 to 8 more minutes. An instant-read thermometer inserted into the center should read at least 71°C (70 degrees C).

pan-roasted-chicken-with-red-pepperwalnu.name: Pan-roasted Chicken With Red Pepper–walnut Spread
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°. Loosen skin on each piece of chicken and rub thyme and garlic underneath it; season with salt and pepper. Heat oil in an ovenproof skillet over medium-high heat. Place chicken skin side down in skillet and cook until golden brown, about 5 minutes. Turn chicken skin side up and transfer skillet to oven. Roast until an instant-read thermometer inserted in thickest part of breast registers 165°, 15–20 minutes.
To serve, slice chicken off the bone and remove skin, if desired. Reserve 4 oz. chicken for Chicken, Cabbage, and Tangerine Salad. Serve pan-roasted chicken with red pepper spread.

pancake-pancake-roll-with-cocoa-lezzet.name: Pancake Pancake Roll With Cocoa

You can sprinkle cocoa instead of powder sugar.
Instead of chocolate sauce, you can use caramel sauce, strawberry sauce or bitter chocolate grater.

pandan-chiffon-cake.name: Pandan Chiffon Cake
instructions: Beat egg whites and cream of tartar in the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with the whisk attachment until stiff peaks form; set aside.
Beat egg yolks and sugar together in a separate bowl until sugar dissolves.
Whisk water and pandan paste together in a separate bowl until smooth; stir into egg yolk mixture. Stir in oil; fold in flour.
Gently fold egg yolk-pandan mixture into egg whites with a spatula; pour into a 9-inch chiffon cake mold.
Bake in the preheated oven until a toothpick inserted into center comes out clean, 45 to 50 minutes.
Cool cake in the pan, inverted onto a wire rack. Run a sharp knife around edges to loosen cooled cake from the mold, invert carefully onto a serving plate.

paneer.name: Paneer
instructions: In a medium saucepan, heat the milk to 91°C (91°C) over medium heat. Using a wooden spoon, stir frequently during the heating process to prevent the milk from scalding. Be sure to scrape the bottom of the pan when you stir because this is where the milk is most prone to sticking. The milk will become foamy and bubbles will form on its surface once it reaches the desired temperature.

Remove from the heat and stir in the lemon juice until evenly distributed, which will cause the separation of curds from the whey. The process should begin right away, but don’t fret if it takes a minute or two. Cover the pan and set aside at room temperature for 10 minutes to let the acid work its magic. Check the mixture at this point: It should be slightly yellow and the curds should have completely separated from the whey. Don’t panic if this hasn’t happened completely yet. Simply add another teaspoon of acid and set aside for a few minutes more. This should do the trick.

Line a fine-mesh sieve with cheesecloth and set the sieve over a bowl. Ladle the curds and whey into the sieve. Reserve the whey for another use. Season the curds (still in the sieve) with salt, stirring it in using a wooden spoon, then gather up the edges of the cheesecloth into a tight bundle and squeeze the excess whey from the curds. Place the cheesecloth and curds on a large plate and shape them into a square. Wrap the cheesecloth edges around the paneer and place a second plate on top of it to compress it. Set aside at room temperature for 30 minutes. At this point the paneer is ready to use or it can be refrigerated in a covered container for up to 3 days.

paneer-and-green-bean-stir-fry.name: Paneer And Green Bean Stir-fry

ingredients.prep: cut into 3x 0.5" pieces (like chunky french fries)

instructions: Stir ¼ cup mirin, 4 tsp. soy sauce, and 2 Tbsp. water in a small bowl to combine; set sauce aside.

Heat 2 Tbsp. vegetable oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high. Arrange one 8–12-oz. block paneer, cut into 3x½" pieces (like chunky french fries), in pan in a single layer and cook, turning occasionally, until golden brown all over, about 5 minutes. Transfer paneer to a plate with tongs.

Add 12 oz. green beans, trimmed, patted dry if needed, to same pan; cook, tossing occasionally and adjusting heat as needed if cooking too quickly, until blistered (but not scorched), 10–12 minutes. Add 3 oz. snow peas; cook, tossing often, until a shade darker and no longer squeaky-raw, 1–2 minutes.

Return paneer to pan; add 1½ tsp. mild curry powder and remaining 1 Tbsp. vegetable oil and cook, tossing constantly, until curry powder is evenly distributed and very fragrant, 1–2 minutes. Add reserved sauce and cook, tossing occasionally to coat, until liquid is evaporated, about 3 minutes. Taste stir-fry and season with kosher salt if needed. Transfer to a platter to serve.

panna-cotta-with-plum-sauce-lezzet.name: Panna Cotta With Plum Sauce

parmesan-peppers.name: Parmesan Peppers

ingredients.item: Kosher salt and freshly gorund black pepper

instructions: Stem, core, and quarter bell peppers; place on a baking sheet. Toss with sliced garlic, thyme, and extra-virgin olive oil; season with kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper. Arrange skin side down in a single layer and roast in a 425° oven until softened, then top with Parmesan and broil until cheese is melted and peppers are slightly charred. Squeeze the lemon juice over peppers.

parsnip-puree.name: Parsnip Purée
instructions: Bring parsnips, garlic, cream, milk, and butter to a boil in a medium saucepan. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer until parsnips are very soft, 10–15 minutes. Uncover and cook until liquid is reduced by half, about 5 minutes; season with salt. Purée in a blender until smooth.
DO AHEAD: Purée can be made 1 day ahead. Let cool; cover and chill. Reheat over medium-low, stirring often.

pascal-veal-hunk-teleculinaria.name: Pascal Veal Hunk
instructions: Prepare the marinade: in a bowl, pour the lemon juice, olive oil, salt and pepper and mix. Place the meat in a container and water with the previous marinade. Add thyme leaves and rosemary and let marinate for about 10 minutes.

Wash the potatoes, cut them in half and put them in a pot, with water, for 20 minutes. Make sure they're cooked and transfer to an oven tray. Season with salt and a thread of olive oil and bring it to the oven, preheated at 180oC, until they are golden.
Remove the meat from the marinade and reserve it. Grate the naco on a very hot grill, until it is at the desired point. Then remove the meat and let it rest on a plate.

In a bowl, mix the marinade with the juices that the meat has released. Add the pepper beans, thyme leaves and rosemary and, if necessary, add some more olive oil and lemon juice. Water the meat with this sauce and serve, sliced, with baked chips.

passionfruit-dessert-lezzet.name: Passionfruit Dessert
instructions: 1. To prepare the interior fill, mix the necessary materials in a bowl and wait on aside until it is ready for dough.
2. Prepare the plum size balls by rolling in your palm when it comes to the inner mortar dough consistency.
3. Mix the soft butter, liquid oil, yogurt and vinegar to a mixing container for the dough.

passover-chocolate-chip-cookies.name: Passover Chocolate Chip Cookies
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Line two baking sheets with parchment paper.
Whisk matzo cake meal, potato starch, and salt together in a bowl.

Beat sugar, margarine, and eggs together in a large bowl with an electric mixer until smooth. Stir in dry ingredients until well combined. Stir in chocolate chips and walnuts. Drop dough by spoonfuls onto the prepared baking sheets.
Working in batches, bake in the preheated oven, switching racks halfway through, until light golden brown, 10 to 15 minutes. Remove from the oven and immediately transfer to wire racks to cool.

pasta-aglio-e-olio-argiro.name: Pasta Aglio E Olio

ingredients.item: Water from what the pasta boiled
A little grated fresh tomato optionally
Water from what the pasta boiled
A little grated fresh tomato optionally

instructions: We start by boiling the pasta first in a large pot with salted boiling water until they become al dente.
In a large deep pan, heat the olive oil and sauté the garlic along with the chopped pepper, for 1-2′, without color.
With tweezers, take the pasta as they drip from the pot and pour them into the oil with the pepper.
Mix very well and add 1 cup of water from the pasta.
Add chopped parsley and two chopped tomatoes.
Mix for 2′ and serve immediately in deep dishes.

pasta-salad-with-mayonnaise-4-irresistib.name: Pasta Salad With Mayonnaise (4 Irresistible Options For Summer)
pasta-salad-with-mayonnaise-4-irresistib.original_name: Ensalada de pasta con mayonesa (4 opciones irresistibles para el verano)

ingredients.item: 300 g of pasta - e.g. fusilli, cotites or farfalle
150 g de guisantes congelados o de lata
3 cucharadas de agua de los pepinillos (si usamos pepinillos)

instructions: Let us cook the dough we have chosen (in our case a type of dough called gemelli), as instructed by the package till it's to the dente. After cooking, drain water and let cool.

While cooking the dough, cook the eggs in another den. To do this, put the four eggs into a small bowl, cover with water and lead to boiling. Once it boils, cook with medium heat for 6 minutes and then cool into cold water.
Meanwhile, scattering maize and peas or cooking them as instructed by the package if they are frozen peas.
To cut ham from York into pits Also Red Pimento, Hard Eggs and, if desired, Jawns from vinegar into small dice.
Add cube-cut ingredients as well as peas and maize to bowl with cold dough and mix everything well.

For dressing, mix mayonnaise with mustard and season with salt and pepper. If you get cheered up you can prepare homemade mayonnaise that will give them a special taste. If we have added pickle to the salad, we add 3-4 tablespoons of water from the pickle to the mayonnaise mixture. Test and adjust the date if necessary
Finally, add dressing to dough and other ingredients. Mix everything well and refrigerate.
Once cold we have our pasta salad with mayonnaise ready to serve.

pastrami-and-potato-hash-with-fried-eggs.name: Pastrami And Potato Hash With Fried Eggs

ingredients.prep: butternut, or kabocha), peeled, cut into 1-inch pieces
white and pale-green parts only, chopped

ingredients.item: Pastrami or any leftover braised meat

instructions: Heat butter and 2 Tbsp. oil in a large skillet over medium-high. Add potatoes and winter squash and season with salt and pepper. Cook, stirring occasionally, until vegetables are tender, 25–30 minutes.

Add leeks and garlic to hash and season with salt and pepper. Using the back of a spoon or a spatula, lightly smash vegetables. Add pastrami and cook, stirring occasionally, until meat is warmed through and flavors have melded, 10–12 minutes.

Heat remaining 2 Tbsp. oil in a medium skillet over medium-high. Carefully crack eggs into skillet one at a time and season with salt and pepper. Cook until whites are set and slightly puffed but yolks are still runny, about 2 minutes.
Divide hash among shallow bowls and top each with an egg; scatter chives over top. Serve with sour cream alongside, if desired.

peach-cheesecake.name: Peach Cheesecake
instructions: Beat cream cheese in a large bowl with an electric mixer until smooth. Gradually beat in confectioners' sugar until fully incorporated.
Add peach preserves and vanilla; beat until combined.
Fold in whipped topping until evenly incorporated and smooth.
Pour 1/2 of the cream cheese mixture into each graham cracker crust and spread evenly. Refrigerate until set, at least 1 hour.
Garnish the top of the cheesecake with fresh peach slices before serving.

peanut-butter-and-chocolate-fudge.name: Peanut Butter And Chocolate Fudge
instructions: Line an 8- or 9-inch square baking pan with parchment paper, leaving 2 to 3 inches overhang on the sides.

Combine milk chocolate chips, semisweet chocolate chips, condensed milk, and salt in a large microwave-safe bowl. Microwave on high until mostly melted, about 2 minutes. Stir with a soft spatula until creamy. Mix in peanut butter and microwave for 1 minute. Add vanilla extract and stir until smooth. Transfer to the prepared baking pan and smooth top with a spatula.

Combine peanut butter chips and milk in a small microwave-safe bowl. Microwave on high for 1 minute. Stir, and continue to microwave in 30 second increments, stirring in between, until mixture is smooth. Pour on top of the fudge mixture and smooth top with a spatula. Place in the refrigerator until firm, about 2 hours.

pecan-pie-with-maple-syrup.name: Pecan Pie With Maple Syrup
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Place a rack in the lowest position.
Mix maple syrup, brown sugar, eggs, melted butter, and vanilla in a large bowl until well blended. Stir in pecans until coated.
Pour batter into the pie shell.
Bake on the lower rack of the preheated oven until the pie shell is golden brown and the filling is set, 35 to 40 minutes.

penne-with-lemon-and-root-vegetables.name: Penne With Lemon And Root Vegetables

ingredients.item: 2x1/2x1/2-inch sticks peeled assorted root vegetables (such as parsnips
ingredients.prep: carrots, celery root, and golden beets)
cut crosswise into 1/2-inch-wide pieces, plus 6 tablespoons chopped celery leaves, divided

ingredients.item: Penne rigate or whole grain penne

instructions: Heat extra-virgin olive oil in large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add vegetables; sprinkle with salt and pepper. Sauté 9 minutes. Using garlic press, squeeze in garlic. Add sliced celery stalks. Sauté vegetables 1 minute longer. Add 1 cup water. Cover and simmer until vegetables are tender, stirring occasionally, 12 to 15 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook pasta in large pot of boiling salted water until just tender but still firm to bite, stirring occasionally. Drain, reserving 1 cup cooking liquid.

Add pasta to vegetables in skillet. Add 3/4 cup reserved cooking liquid, cheese, lemon peel, nutmeg, and 4 tablespoons celery leaves. Toss until heated through and sauce coats pasta, adding more cooking liquid if dry, about 2 minutes. Season to taste with salt and pepper. Transfer to bowls; sprinkle with remaining 2 tablespoons celery leaves.

pepperoni-panzanella.name: Pepperoni Panzanella

ingredients.item: Chopped drained mild peppadew peppers in brine or roasted red peppers

instructions: Preheat oven to 350º. Place bread on a rimmed baking sheet and toast until dry and beginning to turn golden, 10–12 minutes. Transfer bread to a large bowl and add tomato, mozzarella, pepperoni, basil, Peppadew peppers, parsley, oil, and vinegar. Toss to combine and season with salt and pepper. Bread will soften as it absorbs tomato juices, oil, and vinegar.

peppers-stuffed-with-sausage-and-cream-c.name: Peppers Stuffed With Sausage And Cream Cheese
instructions: Preheat the oven to 191°C (190 degrees C). Line a baking sheet with foil.
Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil: add peppers. Off heat, soak peppers until softened, about 5 minutes. Cool.
Meanwhile, combine sausage and cream cheese in a bowl.
Fill peppers with sausage mixture: place onto the prepared baking sheet. Spoon pasta sauce over top: sprinkle with Parmesan cheese.
Bake in the preheated oven until sausage mixture starts to turn golden brown, about 35 minutes. An instant-read thermometer inserted into the center should read at least 71°C (71 degrees C)

peppery-horseradish-mashed-potatoes.name: Peppery Horseradish Mashed Potatoes
instructions: Put potatoes and kosher salt in a large heavy saucepan and generously cover with cold water. Bring to a boil then reduce heat and simmer, partially covered, until potatoes are tender, about 20 minutes. Drain well and return to saucepan.

Meanwhile, heat milk and butter in a small saucepan until butter is melted and milk is hot but not boiling. Add milk mixture, along with horseradish and pepper, to potatoes and mash with a potato masher or fork (to desired consistency). Season with salt and serve warm.

peruvian-corvina-tiradito-what-it-is-and.name: Peruvian Corvina Tiradito, What It Is And How To Prepare It
peruvian-corvina-tiradito-what-it-is-and.original_name: Tiradito de corvina peruano, qué es y cómo se prepara

ingredients.item: 250 g de lomo de corvina muy fresca, sin piel ni espinas
1 / 2 fresh yellow bean or 1 tablespoon of yellow bean paste
1 cucharada de aceite de girasol (o de oliva suave)
1/2 boniato o batata cocida, cortada en cubos

instructions: Let's cook the batata or bonus in water with an anise star for about 20 minutes or till she's tender. We can also cook the bonus into the microwave. Let's have a reservation.
We cut the corvina into thin sheets (sashimi) with a sharp knife. We put them on an elongated plate, without overlap.

For the salsa we mix with the beaker: yellow bean (with no veins and nuggets if fresh), garlic, evaporated milk, lime juice, oil and a pint of salt. We cross till we get a soft and slightly thick cream. If it's very clear, we can add a little more aji or refrigerate a few minutes.
Let's get this yellow ham cream above the fish just before we serve.
We add a few minced cilantro leaves above, some baked corn, pieces of baked potato and, if we like the spicy, some minced red chili above as in the image.

pesto-chicken-thighs-recipe-comedera.name: Pesto Chicken Thighs Recipe
pesto-chicken-thighs-recipe-comedera.original_name: Receta de muslos de pollo al pesto
instructions: Preheat the oven at 180 ° C
To make the pest sauce, put the garlic teeth, pines and basil leaves into a blender or processor.
Loan and add olive oil slowly till a dough forms.
Incorpora el queso parmesano y termina de procesar hasta que tengas el pesto bien integrado.
Sazona los muslos de pollo con sal y pimienta.
Kill the chicken with the pest sauce till they get impregnated. Put them on a plate and baker for 25 minutes or more till they are cooked and juicy.
Once ready, join them with your favorite garrison.

pied-rice-lezzet.name: Pied Rice
instructions: 1. Wait for nohut and barbunya in water in separate pots at a night.
2. Add the chicken water from the water and pour all together.
3. Add rice and bulguru on it.
4. Taste with salt and pepper, and cook the lid in the jealous fire in a closed 20 minutes after adding the butter.
5. Listen to January 5 minutes.
6. Serve.

pineapple-and-gin-frozen-cocktail.name: Pineapple And Gin Frozen Cocktail
instructions: Purée pineapple, gin, simple syrup, lime juice, pineapple juice, and 2 cups ice in a blender until smooth and very thick (mixture will loosen immediately once poured). Divide among glasses and garnish with pineapple wedges and mint sprigs, if desired.

pineapple-mango-habanero-jam.name: Pineapple Mango Habanero Jam
instructions: Inspect 8 half-pint jars for cracks and rings for rust, discarding any defective ones. Immerse in simmering water until jam is ready. Wash new, unused lids and rings in warm soapy water.
Combine mangos, pineapple, and cider vinegar in a stockpot over medium-high heat; simmer. Mash mangos and pineapple to a chunky consistency with a potato masher; stir in sugar until dissolved.

Stir liquid pectin and habanero chile halves into the stockpot. Bring to a rolling boil, stirring frequently. Cook, stirring frequently, until jam thickens, about 2 minutes. Remove chile halves. Discard 6 chile halves; chop 2 halves and stir back into the stockpot.

Pack jam into hot, sterilized jars, filling to within ¼ inch of the top. Run a clean knife or thin spatula around the insides of the jars to remove any air bubbles. Wipe the rims with a moist paper towel to remove any residue. Top with lids and screw rings on tightly.

Place a rack in the bottom of a large stockpot and fill halfway with water. Bring to a boil and lower jars 2 inches apart into the boiling water using a holder. Pour in more boiling water to cover jars by at least 1 inch. Bring to a rolling boil, cover, and process for 10 minutes.

Remove the jars from the stockpot and let rest, several inches apart, for 12 to 24 hours. Press the center of each lid with a finger to ensure the lid does not move up or down. Remove the rings for storage and store in a cool, dark area.

pineapple-paletas.name: Pineapple Paletas
instructions: Bring sugar and 1 cup water to a boil in a small saucepan over high heat, stirring until sugar dissolves. Chill syrup until cold, about 1 hour.

Purée syrup and 2 cups pineapple in a food processor until smooth. Set a fine-mesh strainer over a medium pitcher; strain, pressing on solids to extract purée. Stir in lime juice and remaining 2 cups pineapple. Divide among molds. Cover; insert ice-pop sticks. Freeze until firm. Dip bottoms of molds into hot water for 20-30 seconds to loosen pops. Remove pops and serve.

pineapple-salad-with-prawns-and-avocado-.name: Pineapple Salad With Prawns And Avocado, An Elegant And Light Salad
pineapple-salad-with-prawns-and-avocado-.original_name: Ensalada de piña con langostinos y aguacate, una ensalada elegante y ligera

ingredients.item: ½ mature natural pineapple (about 300 g and clear)
A handful of green leaves (atula, canonigo or pine baby)
½ lime juice (or lemon if you have no)
2 cucharadas de aceite de oliva virgen extra
Seeds of sesame or minced cilantro to decorate (optional)

instructions: We cut pineapple into small dice. If we use natural pineapple, we're trying to get her mature so she doesn't get sour. Let's have a reservation.
Pelamos el aguacate, lo cortamos en dados y lo rociamos con el zumo de lima para que no se oxide.
If the locusts are already cooked, we simply peel them. If they're raw, we cook them with salt for 1 or 2 minutes and we cool them good.

At a salad or fountain, we place the green leaves. We gave up pineapple dice and avocado and locusts. We have some oil and some salt and if we want some pepper. We blend softly so we don't break the avocado. We end up with a touch of roasted sesame or some minced cilantro if we like.

pistachio-mafruka-lezzet.name: Pistachio Mafruka
instructions: 1. For coating, rubbed, sugar, kakule and orange juice thoroughly.
2. Flat the half of the mixture with the die size roller, which you will prepare the sweet between stretch film and place the mold.
3. Cook the milk, semolina, vanilla and sugar in the pot.
4. Published on peanut solid.
5. Place the other half of the peanut mixture in the same way on the muhallebine.
6. slice in square shape by resting in a night refrigerator.
7. Serve with whipped cream and frambuaz.

pizza-oven-dough.name: Pizza Oven Dough
instructions: Mix together water, yeast, and sugar in the bowl of a stand mixer until sugar is dissolved. Add bread flour, olive oil, and salt. Knead using the dough hook attachment until dough is smooth and elastic, about 10 minutes.
Cover dough and refrigerate, 8 hours to overnight.
Punch dough gently to deflate. Divide into 2 pieces on a lightly floured surface. Shape into balls; cover with a damp cloth and let rest, 2 to 3 hours.
Flatten or stretch out dough to desired thickness.

plantain-tarte-tatin.name: Plantain Tarte Tatin

ingredients.prep: peeled and cut diagonally into 1/2-inch slices

instructions: Preheat oven to 191°C (190 degrees C).

Heat an oven-proof skillet over medium heat. Sprinkle white sugar evenly into the bottom of the skillet; cook until sugar melts and turns golden brown, about 1 minute. Reduce heat to low and stir butter into sugar. Stir cinnamon into butter mixture and remove from heat.
Dip each plantain slice into butter mixture to coat on both sides. Arrange coated plantains in a shingled pattern to cover the bottom of the skillet.
Spread puff pastry sheet over plantains, tucking corners into the skillet.

Bake in the preheated oven until puff pastry is golden brown, 30 to 35 minutes. Remove from oven and cool briefly. Place a serving plate upside-down over the top of the skillet; flip the skillet to remove tarte tatin from the pan.

Whisk mascarpone cheese and confectioners' sugar together in a bowl until smooth. Spread a large dollop of mascarpone mixture over the top of the plantains. Serve with remaining mascarpone mixture on the side.

plum-glaze-for-pork.name: Plum Glaze For Pork
instructions: {'step_number': 'Heat the oil in a saucepan over medium heat.', 'description': ''}

plum-pie.name: Plum Pie
instructions: Preheat the oven to 218°C (220 degrees C).
Line the bottom and sides of a 9-inch pie plate with one pie crust. Set second crust aside.
Place plums into a large bowl. Stir in sugar, cornstarch, lemon juice, vanilla, and almond extract with a rubber spatula until well combined. Spoon into a prepared pie crust.

Cover filling with remaining pie crust. Flute the edges to seal or use a fork dipped in egg to press them down. Brush remaining egg on top, then cut several slits in the top crust to allow steam to escape.
Bake in the preheated oven until filling is bubbly and crust is golden brown, 35 to 40 minutes. Let cool for 10 minutes before serving.

poached-pears-argiro.name: Poached Pears

ingredients.item: Flux 1/2 orange cut in stick minutes
Flux 1/2 orange cut in stick minutes

instructions: Choose pears ripe and tightly similar in size.
Clean the peel and leave the stem on the pear.
The pot we use should be small enough to fit the pears.
Add the wine, sugar, cinnamon, orange peel and vanilla to the pot.
Allow to boil over medium heat and mix to melt the sugar.
Cut a small slice of each pear so that they are all the same height.
Put the pears upright on the syrup, with the stem upwards.
Cover the pot and bottle the ears for 15′′.
Turn the ears over and simmer with a lid for about 10-15′ extra without melting.
Remove from the heat and turn the pears back into the syrup.
Leave to stand in the pot until completely cool for about 1 hour.
During their rest, we turn them sometimes into syrup.
When they get cold, we take them with care on a plate.
Remove and strain the syrup.
Add the syrup to the pot and bottle for about 5′ until it is halved and it looks like think honey.
Let it cool.
Serve the orange pears on yogurt.
Pour with the wine syrup and sprinkle with almond fillet.
Garnish with some mint.

pork-chops-with-broccoli-salsa-verde.name: Pork Chops With Broccoli Salsa Verde

ingredients.item: A spice mill or mortar and pestle

instructions: Trim 1" from bottom of broccoli stems, then peel. Remove stalks from crowns; set crowns aside for another use. Cut stalks in half lengthwise and set aside.
Finely grind fennel seeds in spice mill or with mortar and pestle. Transfer to a small bowl and mix in sugar.
Season pork chops all over with salt, then rub with fennel mixture. Let sit at room temperature 30 minutes, or, if you have the time, chill overnight.

Pour 2 Tbsp. oil in a large cast-iron skillet over medium-high. Cook pork chops, without moving around but pressing down with tongs to ensure meat is in direct contact with pan, until golden brown underneath, about 2 minutes. Turn pork chops over and cook until other side is golden brown, about 2 minutes. Continue to cook, turning about every minute or so, until chops are deep golden brown on both sides and an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part registers 135°, 6–8 minutes. Transfer pork chops to a cutting board and let rest 10 minutes before serving. Reserve skillet.

There should still be some oil in reserved skillet; if not, add 1 Tbsp. oil and place skillet over medium-high. Add broccoli stems and cook until lightly charred, about 2 minutes per side. Transfer to a cutting board and let cool 3 minutes, then coarsely chop (about ½" pieces).
Transfer broccoli pieces to a medium bowl and add garlic, olives, parsley, vinegar, lemon zest, and remaining ¼ cup oil. Toss well to combine and season broccoli salsa verde with salt and pepper.
To serve, carve pork chop meat away from bones, then slice meat against the grain ½" thick. Divide slices among plates and spoon some broccoli salsa verde on top.

pork-loin-with-chestnuts-teleculinaria.name: Pork Loin With Chestnuts
instructions: Peel and finely pinch the garlic teeth. Place the pork loin on a tray, season with garlic, sweet pepper, blond leaf, salt, pepper and white wine.
Cut the chestnuts, put them in a pot, cover them with water and bake with a pinch of fennel and salt until you can remove their bark. Drain them, let them simmer and discard the shell. Reserve.

Water the pork loin with the olive oil and bring it to the oven preheated to 180.3 Then add the chestnuts and let it finish baking, also watering with the sauce. If necessary, water with some more water. Remove from the oven and serve.

pork-loin-apples-and-sauerkraut.name: Pork Loin, Apples, And Sauerkraut
instructions: Preheat the oven to 163°C (165 degrees C).

Make the apples and sauerkraut: Mix sauerkraut, apples, onion, and caraway seeds in a large roasting pan. Stir 1/4 cup apple cider and brown sugar together in a separate bowl; pour over sauerkraut mixture.
Make the pork: Stir Thai seasoning, salt, garlic powder, and black pepper together in a small bowl. Rub seasoning mixture all over the top and bottom of the roast.
Make an indentation in the center of the sauerkraut mixture and place seasoned roast into the indentation. Pour remaining 1/4 cup apple cider around roast.

Bake in the preheated oven, basting with the roast's juices after the first hour and then every 30 minutes, until cooked through, 2 1/2 to 3 hours. Add more apple cider if the roast becomes too dry. An instant-read thermometer inserted into the center should read at least 63°C (63 degrees C).

pork-red-na-noodles-and-gravy.name: Pork Red Na (noodles And Gravy)
instructions: Combine marinade ingredients in a large bowl and once whisked together, add pork and marinate overnight.
Soak noodles in warm water before separating out. Drain noodles, then add dark soy sauce and mix around with your hands.

Heat wok or large non-stick pan to medium-high heat. Add 1 tbsp of rapeseed oil, once oil is shimmering, add half the noodles. Fry the noodles until they crisp up in places and char in places. Repeat this with the remaining noodles.
Wipe wok clean with paper towel, add sesame oil. Stir in garlic and soy bean paste. Cook until garlic begins to brown.
Add stock, soy sauce and palm sugar and bring to a boil.
Whisk cornstarch into a glass of water
Add the pork to the boiling sauce (must be boiling so the coating on the pork doesn’t come off)
And simmer for one minute before adding the broccoli.
Simmer for 3-4 more minutes.
Stir in corn starch mix, cook off until sauce thickens and glistens.
Serve over the rice noodles, adding chilli and shallots to garnish.

pork-steaks-with-cream-sauce-and-farfall.name: Pork Steaks With Cream Sauce And Farfalle
pork-steaks-with-cream-sauce-and-farfall.original_name: Bifinhos de porco com molho de natas e farfalle

ingredients.item: Q.b.  sal e mistura de 5 pimentas

instructions: Cooks the dough in boiling water seasoned with salt, then drains it, passes it by cold water and lets it run again. Have it on an oven tray and book.

Season the meat with half the chopped garlic, lemon juice, salt and pepper. Light a large and large frying pan with the olive oil, let it warm, add the steaks and fry them both sides. Remove and book next to the dough.
In the same skillet, praise the remaining garlic and onions, previously minced. Season with salt, paprika and basil and wrap. Remove a part of the saute and place it on the dough.
Mantendo ao lume a frigideira com o restante refogado, adicione as natas, deixe ferver, depois retifique os temperos e retire do lume.
Water the steaks with the cream sauce, sprinkle with grated cheese, spread over some cherry tomatoes cut in half and light in the oven, preheated at 200°C, just to gratin a little.

pork-stew-in-green-salsa-guisado-de-puer.name: Pork Stew In Green Salsa (guisado De Puerco Con Tomatillos)

ingredients.prep: trimmed of excess fat and cut into 1-inch cubes

instructions: Combine flour, pepper, 1 teaspoon salt, and cumin in a large bowl. Dredge pork in flour mixture until well coated.

Heat olive oil in a large, heavy pan or Dutch oven over medium-high heat until shimmers. Add pork, in batches, in a single layer; brown on all sides, about 15 minutes. Transfer pork to a bowl; cover to keep warm.

Add onion to pan; cook and stir over medium heat, adding more olive oil if necessary, until translucent and beginning to brown, about 7 minutes. Return pork to pan; stir in tomatillos, water, canned chiles, 1/2 cup cilantro, jalapeños, garlic, and marjoram. Check seasoning; add 1 pinch salt, if needed. Cover and simmer over low heat, stirring occasionally, until pork tender, about 1 hour. Degrease stew before serving in bowls, garnished with sour cream and cilantro.

pound-cake-bread-pudding-with-leftover-h.name: Pound Cake Bread Pudding With Leftover Halloween Candy
instructions: Butter an 8-inch square baking dish. Place pound cake chunks in the prepared baking dish and set aside.

Beat eggs and sugar together in a large bowl until frothy. Add milk, heavy cream, vanilla bean paste, and salt; beat for 1 to 2 minutes more. Stir in chopped candy, breaking up any clumps that may form.
Pour egg mixture over pound cake chunks; press cake into mixture to submerge. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for 2 hours to overnight.
Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C).
Bake in the preheated oven until puffed and set, about 1 hour. Let bread pudding cool on a wire rack for 15 to 20 minutes before serving.

preserved-lemon-and-tomato-chicken.name: Preserved Lemon And Tomato Chicken
instructions: Pat 4 skinless, boneless chicken breasts (about 1½ lb.) dry with paper towels, then season on both sides with kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper.

Heat 2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil in a medium high-sided skillet over medium-high. Add breasts and cook until golden brown, about 5 minutes per side. Transfer to a plate (they won’t be cooked all the way through at this point).

Reduce heat to medium. Drizzle a little oil into same pan, add 1 lb. cherry tomatoes (about 1¼ pints), and season with salt. Cook, tossing occasionally, until blistered, about 2 minutes. Add 1 preserved lemon, seeds removed, finely chopped, and 4 garlic cloves, thinly sliced, and cook, scraping up any browned bits stuck to bottom of pan and pressing down on tomatoes to help them burst, until combined and fragrant, about 2 minutes.

Add 2 Tbsp. double-concentrated tomato paste and ¼ tsp. crushed red pepper flakes. Cook, stirring often, until paste deepens in color, about 2 minutes. Stir in 1½ cups water and bring to a simmer. Cook until slightly thickened, about 5 minutes.

Add 4 sprigs oregano or 2 tsp. dried oregano and chicken breasts along with any juices from plate and cook, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking, until sauce is thickened and chicken is cooked through, 10–12 minutes. Remove from heat and stir in 1 Tbsp. fresh lemon juice. Serve saucy chicken with steamed couscous.

prosciutto-watercress-and-fontina-toasti.name: Prosciutto, Watercress, And Fontina Toasties
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°. Bake prosciutto on a parchment-lined baking sheet until it darkens and shrinks slightly, 6–8 minutes. Let cool (meat will crisp as it cools).
Top 4 slices of bread with half of cheese, prosciutto (breaking to fit), watercress, then remaining cheese (it will bind as it melts) and bread; butter tops.

Heat a large skillet over medium heat. Working in 2 batches, cook sandwiches, butter side down, pressing firmly, until bottom is golden brown. Butter tops, turn, and cook until golden brown and cheese is melted, about 4 minutes. Transfer to a wire rack; let cool slightly. Remove crusts and cut each sandwich into 4 triangles.
DO AHEAD: Toasties can be cooked 2 hours ahead. Store uncovered at room temperature. Reheat before serving.

puff-pastry-contractors-argiro.name: Puff Pastry Contractors
instructions: We work on the puff pastry while it's cold.
We open the puff pastry on the work bench.
Rub it all over the surface with the beaten egg.
Sprinkle with sugar.
With a pizza peach or very sharp knife cut the dough in 5 or 6 strips, depending on the size we want to give the cake.
Vertically cut into smaller pieces the size of a contractor.
Transfer to an anti-stick baking pan and bake in a well-heated oven at 200oC for about 15 minutes until golden and puffy.
Let them cool well.
If we want to fill them with praline or jam.
The puffing up inside makes a gap.
Serve them as breakfast as snacks or as sweet with our coffee.
They are crispy and irresistible with buttery, fluffy and preserved puff pastry.

puff-pastry-pizza-with-cheese-and-ham-te.name: Puff Pastry Pizza With Cheese And Ham

Spread the tomato sauce on the surface, leaving a margin all around. Lay the onion on top, season it with salt, pepper and a string of olive oil and put it in the oven for about 15 minutes, or until the dough is very crispy. Take it off and let it cool.
Cover the pizza with the splattered feta cheese and the slightly wrapped ham slices. Sprinkle with ground black pepper and serve.

pumpkin-torte.name: Pumpkin Torte
instructions: Preheat oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Lightly grease one 9x13 inch pan.
In a medium bowl, mix the graham cracker crumbs and margarine. Evenly press into prepared pan to form a crust.
Blend together the cream cheese, eggs and sugar; evenly spread over crust and bake for 20 minutes in the preheated oven. Set aside to cool.
In a 2 quart saucepan, combine the pumpkin, egg yolks, milk, 3/4 cup sugar, cinnamon, nutmeg and salt. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until sugar is dissolved. Set aside to cool.
Combine gelatin and cold water in a small bowl and allow to soften for 5 minutes. Mix in boiling water to dissolve. Stir into the pumpkin mixture.

In a mixing bowl, beat egg whites until frothy. Gradually add 1/2 cup sugar; beat until stiff. Fold into pumpkin mixture and pour over cream cheese layer. Refrigerate until firm, about 4 hours, and cut into squares. Top with whipped topping and sprinkle with walnuts.
